key_insight: A microstructure-aware convolutional neural network can bridge atomistic MD and continuum FE to predict shock-to-detonation behavior in energetic composites without relying on traditional scale separation.
engineering_relevance: This enables parameter-free, multiscale prediction of hotspot formation, deflagration, and run-to-detonation in nanostructured energetic materials, improving design and safety analysis.
method: Train MISTnetX on large-scale molecular dynamics shock simulations with complex microstructures.
Use the network to provide sub-grid shock–microstructure, thermal, and chemistry information to continuum finite-element models.
Apply the coupled MD-to-FE workflow to a synthetic nanostructured plastic-bonded RDX composite.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that MISTnetX was trained on MD shock-propagation data and successfully applied to predict the full run-to-detonation transition in a realistic synthetic composite.
watch_item: The demonstrated application is on a synthetic but realistic RDX composite, so broader validation across different energetic formulations and microstructure classes is still needed.
paper_title: Microstructure-Aware Deep Learning Bridges Atomistics to Macroscale for Shock-to-Detonation Prediction
